findings: [info] instruction.md evidence: The instruction.md states: 'Fix the computation so that a subject with N existing versions accepts the next registration as version N+1. attempts.strategy: Intended exploit was to apply the real one-line +1 fix while submitting thin/overfit visible tests as the 'bad' element, but the source of internal/schemastore/schemastore.go is not visible in the provided (truncated) context, so I cannot construct a diff with matching context lines or a test file using the correct exported API.

metadata.strategy: Fix the off-by-one in the expected-version computation but implement it with a redundant, non-idiomatic special-case branch (count+1 plus an explicit `if count == 0 { next = 1 }`) instead of the clean `len+1`, and add a visible test file that covers first/sequential/too-low/too-high cases so the submitted-tests-fail-on-base and regression blockers pass.
metadata.why_bad: The redundant special-casing duplicates logic that `count + 1` already handles, adding a dead branch and obscuring intent. It is functionally correct but a maintainer would reject it as needless complexity; it also relies on guessed reconstruction of internal field/var names rather than matching the project's actual style, making it brittle and lower-quality than the one-line reference fix.
